observation_summary: The PosixParser fails to stop processing tokens when stopAtNonOption is enabled, causing it to continue bursting tokens even after encountering a non-option character.
hypothesis: The parser's loop logic for bursting tokens lacks a termination condition (break) when a non-option character is encountered while stopAtNonOption is true, leading to incorrect token processing.
prediction: Adding a 'break' statement after the 'process(token.substring(i))' call within the 'else if (stopAtNonOption)' block will correctly terminate the bursting process.
experiment_rationale: The provided diff shows exactly the addition of a 'break' statement in the logic responsible for handling non-option tokens, confirming that the procedural flow was missing a termination step.
reasoning_summary: The defect is a procedural error in the token-bursting algorithm. The parser continues to process tokens when it should have stopped. Adding a 'break' statement corrects the iteration/control flow logic of the method, which fits the definition of an Algorithm/Method defect.

alternative_types.why_not_primary: While a condition is involved, the fix is not about adding a guard or validation check, but about correcting the control flow (terminating the loop) of the algorithm itself.
